title: Asuka Langley Soryu Long Analysis HPD Validation
display_title: Asuka Langley Soryu Long Analysis HPD Validation
wikitext:
[]: Asuka Langley Soryu: Long Analysis, HPD, Validation             Quote Post by flutflutflyer » Mon Jun 09, 2014 10:24 pm Since my friend from tumblr ever so kindly asked that I transferred his post here (and then I realized that I had completely forgotten my log-in after years of not using my EG account), here it is, taken from here. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Mon Jun 09, 2014 10:54 pm Good Sir, that was probably one of the most in-depth, thought provoking interpretations I have ever read, tell your friend Great Job

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Mon Jun 09, 2014 11:58 pm Holy crap! This is awesome!

[]:             Quote Post by Coldwire » Tue Jun 10, 2014 12:23 am Nice analysis, I disagree with a few things but overall it was good. In regards to Shinji appearing at Asuka's mindrape, I just wanted to point out that his expression, I think, is one of annoyance, now, I'm not saying he is jealous or anything like that, but he's thinking something along the lines of "there she goes again, doesn't she get bored of doing that all the time". I think it's a memory of her, she saw him make that face that night while she was clinging to Kaji. I think the analysis downplays Asuka's feelings towards Shinji. Now, I don't think they loved each other at any point of the series (even though I ship them, yeah you got me) but I think there is more to her feelings for him rather than just being a bad substitute for Kaji. Good job anyways, the effort is appreciated.

[]: Re: Asuka Langley Soryu: Long Analysis, HPD, Validation             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jun 10, 2014 1:54 am Excellent analysis, in all (as far as the first post went, that is). Just a couple of nits, mainly about the missing years. I'm not sure that there are any suggestions that she had boyfriends in Germany -- or really friends of any sort. OTOH her ego would have been flattered by years of being NERV Germany's special girl, pretty much the whole raison d'etre for that outpost -- going to being the outsider and no longer unique was the first of the emotional props to be removed. flutflutflyer wrote: and then I realized that I had completely forgotten my log-in after years of not using my EG account

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Tue Jun 10, 2014 7:31 am Well done, sir. Very well done indeed.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Tue Jun 10, 2014 9:20 am Great post!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jun 10, 2014 12:53 pm The original tumblr post got cut short by the post size limit, so here's the rest SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jun 10, 2014 1:38 pm This is a decent analysis. You're spot on regarding her HPD, and her thirst for validation is properly explored. However, there are some big missteps throughout: Asuka's boyfriends: Between her treatment of her classmates' affections and her rather obvious lack of experience with boys I think the notion of her having boyfriends in Germany is far-fetched. You're basing this on a single line from Kaji, and I've never read it the way you seem to have. Misato's sexuality: It is heavily implied, during her sex scene with Kaji, that Misato has never been with anyone else. Asuka no doubt thinks she sleeps around with everyone, and Misato does feel like she uses her sexuality to influence men, but apart from Kaji she's chaste as can be. Episode 9: I think your assessment of Asuka's motivations here is way off. She doesn't want Shinji to call her bluff just so she can shoot him down, she actually wants to be held. This is confirmed in the DC material in episode 22'. Also, there's no indication she intentionally layed down beside Shinji; she appears to have been sleepwalking, or was simply out of it. Remember that for the rest of the week she'd been sleeping in that very room. The kiss: This was also problematic, in that I think you pushed the validation angle way too far here. Curiosity and experimentation are a thing, you know? The way you describe her here makes her seem far more calculating than I think she probably is, and I think the situation was a lot more complex than you indicate here. The ending: I think you've got Asuka completely backwards here. She did not learn to love herself during that final battle; it was a false rebirth, not a true rebirth. Instead, her desperation gave Kyoko the push she needed to reach out to her, and that finally gave Asuka the external validation she'd always desired -- but that isn't what she really needs. It was her ultimate retreat into the world of Eva, the world Anno's clearly telling us we must escape, and that is why she ultimately fell. She is screaming at Shinji in the kitchen because she's in agony and is insulted that he is attempting to use her instead of actually trying to help. But that aside, you did a good job. I especially liked the notes about Asuka reaching out to Rei, and the fact you pointed out that she doesn't go looking for conflict. This is why I maintain she's not a bully, even if she is abusive: she doesn't actually hate Rei or Shinji, and when she's not feeling threatened she actually does want to help them out. But she usually does feel threatened, for all the reasons you indicate, and as such lashes out in self-defense. That is the product of fear, not malice or cruelty, which means the crucial element central to all bullying -- the intent to victimize and cause harm -- simply is not there. But I suspect people are tired of that argument, so I won't belabor the point. Once again, nice work!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jun 10, 2014 4:53 pm Very good analysis, although I partially disagree on the Shinji-Asuka dynamic during pre-Instrumentality : First as Bagheera noted because Asuka's "rebirth" during the final battle was false, an ultimate return to the metaphorical womb by giving herself entirely to her destructive instincts and the Eva, besides the tumblr's assessment would make her be really dickish in the Hell Kitchen scene : as it noted, following the assessment she finally got past her need for validation, Asuka understood Shinji's pain, who didn't had received any help (yet) in overcoming his own issues, yet instead of trying to help him get his epiphany like she did thanks to her mother, or at least tell him that he's doing the wrong thing and tell him what he should do, she took the opportunity to viciously tear him down and positioning herself as superior, all because she got external help before him, and Rei actually called her on it, as you noted. That why I think that during the Hell Kitchen scene, Asuka was still as screwed up as Shinji, and that she got her own little therapy herself partially off-screen to emerge in the final scene liberated from her demons, like Shinji. Secondly, I think that the analysis vilify too much Shinji : sure what he did in the hospital was disgusting and repulsive, but I think that the article widely missed the mark in interpreting Shinji's action as him consciously using her as object of sexual pleasure, and later killing her as a simple stepping stone to be one with Kaworu, although that would make a disturbing and awesome parallel with Gendo's treatment of the Akagis as simple stepping stones to be reunited with Yui and object of sexual pleasure when he got his sexual urges, and who later dispose of them when they aren't useful anymore. Personally, I think that the article is trying too hard to find a deliberate and calculated motive behind Shinji's action, while in my opinion it was much simpler and "animalistic" : at the point of EoE Shinji lost (literally or figuratively) pretty much everyone who could give him some desperately needed emotional support (remember that the day before he had to kill Kaworu himself, making him kill an human for the first time even though in soul he was an Angel) and in an half-crazed trance sought comfort in any possible form, leading to this infamous act in the hospital. To make a bold comparison, it's like when two people are stranded somewhere without food, and after a few days one of them goes into a crazy state and sinks to cannibalism to satiate his hunger, something horrible, but that the person would never do under normal circumstances (unless he's Hannibal Lecter, but that's a whole different story) Also, the article says that Kaworu was Shinji's true final objective in triggering Instrumentality, yet he only appeared near the end of Instrumentality, when Shinji was already beginning to realize that Instrumentality is a false happiness and thinking of destroying it, the one whom Shinji reunited with and interacted the most during Instrumentality was Rei, who served as his guide and "therapist" inside it. But maybe that's my bias against tumblr speaking, especially when I saw the terms "trigger warning" and "cis male and female" creeping into the analysis! On a side note, I find it really fascinating how previously to EoE, Shinji was the one with the moral high ground : he was the nice if shy and damaged guy that tried to understand Asuka but was rejected because of the later issues before entering in a self-destructive spiral, and how with that single five minute long scene in the hospital, the tables were completely turned and Asuka ended with the moral high ground and Shinji as "the lowest of the lows".

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jun 10, 2014 5:04 pm ElMariachi wrote: Secondly, I think that the analysis vilify too much Shinji : sure what he did in the hospital was disgusting and repulsive, but I think that the article widely missed the mark in interpreting Shinji's action as him consciously using her as object of sexual pleasure, and later killing her as a simple stepping stone to be one with Kaworu, which would make a disturbing and awesome parallel with Gendo's treatment of the Akagis as simple stepping stones to be reunited with Yui and object of sexual pleasure when he got his sexual urges, and who later dispose of them when they aren't useful anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Tue Jun 10, 2014 8:16 pm Struggled to bring myself to read it when I noticed the "trigger warning" bullshit. Anyone who needs trigger warnings shouldn't be watching Evangelion. Also it was so damn long...a concise version would help. I did notice that the whole thing seemed to be trying as hard as possible to portray Shinji as this woman-hating sex-fiend psycho, such as the bit at the end describing Shinji as "Violent and misogynistic". I read this and thought "No. Just no." Shinii isn't a misogynist, even in EoE (and definitely not in TV series). In EoE he's completely broken and his mind is in ruins- just look at that haunted, empty stare when he notices Asuka on the beach. That's not the face of a wife-beating thug. It's the face of a shell-shocked husk of a human who has had his very soul vivisected, merged with the rest of humanity and then stitched back together (not to mention the implication he'd been stuck completely on his own for a long time). He doesn't choke Asuka because "muh patriarchy" or out of some hatred of women, but because he literally had no idea what to do- he's been utterly traumatized, and seeing the return of Asuka, the one who ultimately (probably unintentionally) convinced him that nobody wanted him, was enough to probably push him to total despair- he thinks she's come back to torment him and make what's left of his no doubt short life even more of a misery. Obviously this wasn't Asuka's intent (she was just determined to survive and as such crawled out of the sea of LCL) but to a 14 year old boy who has had his very essence ripped apart and reconstituted as the locus of a gigantic apocalypse ritual befoee being possibly left completely alone for at least a week amongst the ruins of humanity... It's also worth noting that in both the hospital scene and the kitchen scene, he certainly doesn't sound like a "misogynist". In both situations he's pleading for help . In the hospital scene, before...that happens, he's begging Asuka to wake up- the sight of her completely sedated and (as far as he can tell) practically dead is gut-wrenching for him. He doesn't want her to suffer, to be reduced to a mere shell of her former glory, and seeing her like this makes him feel like he's failed. I'd agree with Bagheera's idea that the...act in question is him trying to justify his self-hatred- "I've become a monster, why should I stop myself?" which of course simply hurts him more. However I'd argue it isn't just his execution of Kaworu that convinced him he was a monster, and that he actually felt guilt about Asuka's mind rape in 22, believing that it was his fault. He's grown up believing he should shoulder all the blame for everything, and doing so eventually breaks him. In the kitchen scene, he once again is begging Asuka to help him, to give him some reason to live on, believing she is the last person that cares about him. Her refusal to help him is what breaks him- his last light has been snuffed out. It's worth noting that when he strangles her, Asuka isn't just Asuka, or some representation of women- she represents the entire world, which Shinji is convinced has abandoned him. He thinks that the world hates him, and so he symbolically destroys the world by throttling Asuka. He isn't the vengeful husband that visits bloody violence upon his dissatisfied wife, he is the lost child casting down the world that has left him to rot. His role in the scene (and the rest of the film) isn't misogynistic, it's utterly nihilistic, a desire to reduce himself and the rest of the world to nothingness. I also take issue with the whole "Anyone can return from the LCL" thing. Yes, they can, but they probably won't. The entire world was basically mindraped into primordial soup, and most people probably don't have the willpower to try and reform their broken minds. Asuka is unique in that her willpower is monumental- most of the human race are probably unable or unwilling to reform. Also bear in mind that it is implied that it took weeks for Asuka to return, by which point Shinji's mind was a total wreck. By the time anyone else they know emerges, Asuka and Shinji would probably have died. And even if they do return, they'll have beeb merged with Shinji's mind as he decided to unmake humanity- he sure ain't gonna be popular. In fact he'd probably be murdered by some psycho who crawled from the LCL to avenge himself upon the one that destroyed the world. Of course I am quite unique on this forum in that I not only consider EoE to be a totally grim and hopeless ending but also consider it non canon in its entirety, so happily disregard what I say.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jun 10, 2014 8:34 pm Squigsquasher wrote: Struggled to bring myself to read it when I noticed the "trigger warning" bullshit. Anyone who needs trigger warnings shouldn't be watching Evangelion. Also it was so damn long...a concise version would help.

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Wed Jun 11, 2014 2:07 am Continued nice work on the analysis. Let's just take a closer look at a few things and-- Shinji is violent and misogynistic

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jun 11, 2014 3:29 am I ticked too at the "Shinji is violent and misogynistic" part, Shinji never showed any hate for women in particular, in fact the only person he came close to hate with all his might was his father, a man.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Wed Jun 11, 2014 2:47 pm I think it's "his actions towards Asuka were, at times, misogynistic" rather than "misogyny is an intrinsic part of Shinji's character". One diminishes him as a character; the other doesn't.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jun 11, 2014 4:16 pm ^ But even then, Shinji's action weren't misogynistic : he didn't attacked Asuka because she's a girl .

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jun 11, 2014 4:26 pm ElMariachi wrote: ^ But even then, Shinji's action weren't misogynistic : he didn't attacked Asuka because she's a girl .

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Jun 11, 2014 4:42 pm Am I the only one on whose screen the whole analysis isn't displayed? It stops right before the part about EoE. Whatever, I read it on tumblr. Great stuff, love it. Just don't agree with the part that Asuka had several boyfriends. It's neither alluded nor outright denied, but I still think you're jumping to conclusions there. Other than that

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jun 11, 2014 4:45 pm Same here - in Opera 12, which is sometimes cranky, for all its virtues; I haven't checked other browsers. Frankly, given the link, there was no need for the copy anyway.

Asuka is unique in that her willpower is monumental- most of the human race are probably unable or unwilling to reform. Also bear in mind that it is implied that it took weeks for Asuka to return, by which point Shinji's mind was a total wreck. By the time anyone else they know emerges

Article: Asuka Langley Soryu Long Analysis HPD Validation
 of the human race are probably unable or unwilling to reform. Also bear in mind that it is implied that it took weeks for Asuka to return, by which point Shinji's mind was a total wreck. By the time anyone else they know emerges, Asuka and Shinji would probably have died. And even if they do return, they'll have beeb merged with Shinji's mind as he decided to unmake humanity- he sure ain't gonna be popular. In fact he'd probably be murdered by some psycho who crawled from the LCL to avenge himself upon the one that destroyed the world. Of course I am quite unique on this forum in that I not only consider EoE to be a totally grim and hopeless ending but also consider it non canon in its entirety, so happily disregard what I say.
